statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

articles.headline: Collier back at practice, looks to boost red-hot Miles, Lynx
articles.description: Minnesota Lynx star Napheesa Collier has returned to team practice and activities, keeping her recovery progression from surgeries on both ankles on track as expected.

images.name: Napheesa Collier [600x400]
images.caption: Napheesa Collier is back with the Minnesota Lynx for team practice and activities after surgeries on her right and left ankles, keeping her on track in her progress to return to help keep Olivia Miles and company atop the WNBA standings.

articles.headline: WNBA All-Star 2026 starters predictions: Clark, Wilson, more
articles.description: The 2026 WNBA All-Star Game will be played in Chicago on July 25. These players should be on the court at tip-off.

articles.description: Atlanta Dream (12-7, 7-2 Eastern Conference) at Washington Mystics (9-9, 6-5 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: How the Liberty beat the Aces for the Commissioner's Cup title
articles.description: Sabrina Ionescu and Breanna Stewart led New York past Las Vegas for the 2026 WNBA Commissioner's Cup title.

images.name: Liberty defeat Aces to win the Commissioner's Cup
images.caption: Liberty defeat Aces to win the Commissioner's Cup

articles.headline: 'Job done,' says Stewart, as Liberty beat Aces to win 2nd WNBA Cup title
articles.description: Sabrina Ionescu scored 26 points, Breanna Stewart added 25 and the New York Liberty beat the Las Vegas Aces 93-85 to win the WNBA Commissioner's Cup on Tuesday night.

images.name: Liberty defeat Aces to win the Commissioner's Cup
images.caption: Liberty defeat Aces to win the Commissioner's Cup

articles.headline: New York Liberty beat the Las Vegas Aces 93-85 to win the Commissioner's Cup
articles.description: — Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionsecu wouldn't let the New York Liberty come away without the Commissioner's Cup championship in front of their home crowd.

images.name: Sabrina Ionescu knocks down the dagger 3
images.caption: Sabrina Ionescu knocks down the dagger 3

articles.headline: Alyssa Thomas: Received death threats over Caitlin Clark incident
articles.description: Mercury forward Alyssa Thomas said she has received death threats and been called racial slurs following last week's in-game incident with Fever star Caitlin Clark.

articles.headline: Kawhi Leonard is the latest star to return to old stomping grounds
articles.description: Kawhi Leonard joins the likes of LeBron James, Ken Griffey Jr. and Zdeno Chara in returning to a former team.

images.name: kawhi return [608x342]
images.caption: Kawhi Leonard won a championship with the Toronto Raptors during the 2018-19 season.

images.name: lebron james [608x342]
images.caption: James delivered one of the most iconic moments in NBA history when he brought a championship to Cleveland after returning to his hometown team.

images.name: allen iverson [608x342]
images.caption: Iverson played in 25 games during his second stint with the Philadelphia 76ers in the 2009-10 season.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: Charles Woodson [608x342]
images.caption: Woodson started and ended his career an Oakland Raider -- via two separate stints with the team.

images.name: randy moss 2010 [608x342]
images.caption: Moss played four games with the Vikings during the 2010 season.
images.credit: (Photo by David Bergman /Sports Illustrated via Getty Images)

images.name: albert pujols 2022 [608x342]
images.caption: Pujols hit his 700th career home run during his second stint with the Cardinals.

images.name: ken griffey jr. 2009 [608x342]
images.caption: Griffey returned to Seattle after the franchise dealt him to Cincinnati in 2000.

images.name: dewanna bonner [608x342]
images.caption: Bonner returned to the Mercury after the Fever waived her earlier this season.

images.name: zdeno chara [608x342]
images.caption: Chara played the first and last games of his NHL career with the New York Islanders.
images.credit: (Photo by Mike Stobe/NHLI via Getty Images)

images.name: keenan allen for trending [608x342]
images.caption: Keenan Allen spent his first 11 NFL seasons with the Chargers.

images.name: Nneka Ogwumike [1296x729] - Copy
images.caption: Nneka Ogwumike is the Sparks' career leader in win shares.

articles.headline: LeBron James, Serena Williams lead list of players who remained great late in their careers
articles.description: James and Williams are still competing at a high level late in their careers -- and other notable athletes such as Tom Brady, Barry Bonds, Sue Bird and more can relate.

images.name: serena williams wimbledon [608x342]
images.caption: Serena Williams of the United States during her Ladies' Singles match against Maya Joint of Australia on Day 2 of the 2026 Wimbledon Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club, London.
images.credit: Andrew Matthews/PA Images via Getty Images

images.name: Tom Brady [608x342] - Copy
images.caption: Tom Brady won four of his rings after the age of 37.

images.name: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar (vs. Mavericks, 1986) [608x342] - Copy
images.caption: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's sky hook was unstoppable right up until his retirement.

images.name: Sue Bird [608x342]
images.caption: Sue Bird was dishing out assists even in the later stages of her career.

images.name: Barry Bonds 2002 World Series [608x342] - Copy (3)
images.caption: Barry Bonds holds the record for the most home runs hit by an MLB player in a single season.

images.name: Wayne Gretzky (Rangers) 180622 [608x342] - Copy
images.caption: Wayne Gretzky didn't stop being "The Great One" in the twilight of his career.

images.name: Rich Eisen wonders what's next for LeBron after he told Lakers they can move on
images.caption: Rich Eisen wonders what's next for LeBron after he told Lakers they can move on

articles.headline: What is the WNBA Commissioner's Cup? Format, winners, stats
articles.description: The WNBA Commissioner's Cup awards a trophy to a team in the middle of the season. Learn more about the format, rules, and previous winners now.

images.name: Breanna Stewart was named the 2026 Commissioner's Cup MVP [608x342]
images.caption: Breanna Stewart was named the 2026 Commissioner's Cup MVP
images.alt: Breanna Stewart was named the 2026 Commissioner's Cup MVP

images.name: Natasha Howard #6 of the Indiana Fever [608x342]
images.caption: Natasha Howard was named the 2025 Commissioner's Cup MVP.

articles.headline: WNBA Commissioner's Cup 2026: Keys to Aces-Liberty final
articles.description: New York hosts Las Vegas on Tuesday in a clash between the WNBA's winningest teams over the past three seasons.

images.name: Jackie Young and Pauline Astier [1296x729]
images.caption: New York rookie Pauline Astier is averaging 10.1 points and 3.8 assists this season and had seven points, two assists and no turnovers in 20 minutes in a win over Las Vegas and Jackie Young on June 23.

images.name: NaLyssa Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: NaLyssa Smith is having her best season statistically in three years, but her numbers were subpar in a loss to New York on June 23.

images.name: Las Vegas Aces vs. New York Liberty - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Las Vegas Aces vs. New York Liberty, 06/23/2026

articles.byline: Kareem Copeland and Kendra Andrews and Michael Voepel

articles.headline: Aces' Wilson ruled out of Commissioner's Cup championship game
articles.description: A'ja Wilson has been ruled out of the Las Vegas Aces' matchup Tuesday against the New York Liberty in the Commissioner's Cup championship game due to a right leg injury.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1024x576]
images.caption: A'ja Wilson is listed as questionable ahead of the Aces' Commissioner Cup championship matchup against the New York Liberty.

article.headline: Nogic scores 27 points, Mercury blow big lead before topping Sky 91-83
article.description: — Jovana Nogic scored 27 points and the Phoenix Mercury topped the Chicago Sky 91-83 on Friday night after blowing a 17-point lead in the second half.
article.linkText: Nogic scores 27 points, Mercury blow big lead before topping Sky 91-83

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Phoenix Mercury vs. Chicago Sky - Game Highlights) 2026/05/16 ESHEET

article.story: PHOENIX -- — Jovana Nogic scored 27 points and the Phoenix Mercury topped the Chicago Sky 91-83 on Friday night after blowing a 17-point lead in the second half. Alyssa Thomas and Kahleah Copper both had 17 points for the Mercury (2-2). They combined with Nogic, a 28-year-old rookie from Serbia, for the final seven points, four rebounds and two steals after the Sky (2-1) pulled within one point at 84-83 with 2:09 to play. Natasha Mack had 10 points for Phoenix, which went 37 of 41 from the foul line on coach Nate Tibbetts' 49th birthday. Rickea Jackson scored 29 points for Chicago, which was 19 of 23 from the foul line. Rookie Gabriela Jaquez had 13 points, but Kamilla Cardoso was held to seven. Skylar Diggins, who was averaging 18 points, failed to score before an eye injury ended her night in second quarter. The Sky took their first lead at 75-73 on Jackson's 3-pointer with less than six minutes to play. She also gave Chicago an 80-79 lead with 3:49 to go. The Mercury led 45-35 at halftime after going up 45-30 when Nogic made a free throw following a technical foul on Chicago coach Tyler Marsh. Phoenix pushed the lead to 17 at 54-37, but the Sky trimmed the deficit to 69-59 at the end of the third quarter. Up next Sky: Cap their season-opening four-game road trip at Minnesota on Sunday. Mercury: Play their third straight home game against Toronto on Tuesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
